title: The Illustrated Guide to a PhD
note: A beautifully simple visual explainer on what a PhD really means — and why it matters beyond academia.

title: You and Your Research — Richard Hamming
note: Hamming's famous 1986 talk on what separates good scientists from great ones. Timeless and brutally honest.

title: Maker's Schedule, Manager's Schedule
note: Paul Graham's classic on why interruptions destroy deep work — and how to protect your best hours.

title: The Bus Ticket Theory of Genius
note: An unconventional take on what drives breakthrough ideas: obsessive, seemingly useless interest in a thing.

title: How to Do Great Work
note: Paul Graham's most comprehensive essay on curiosity, taste, and the conditions that produce exceptional output.
